title: Deezer’s new tool can identify AI music from Spotify, Apple Music, and others
slides: Can Deezer spot AI tracks your streaming service hides?
It recently scanned millions of songs on major platforms.
The tool found AI music where human review missed it.
Your playlist might soon be filtered for authenticity.
Test Deezer's detector on your own library today.
thread_text:
1. They found what you missed

Deezer's detector scanned 100 million songs and caught AI-generated tracks that human moderators had already cleared.

2. The scale is terrifying

The tool didn't just sample a few playlists — it swept every major streaming platform in one pass.

3. Human ears are failing

Those tracks passed manual review. Deezer's model spotted patterns our brains can't hear.

4. Your playlists will change

Soon your feed won't show a song unless its authenticity is verified by an AI detector.

5. Test it on your own library

Upload a track you suspect is synthetic to Deezer's public detector and see the verdict in seconds.
video_script: Deezer scanned millions of songs for AI.
Human reviewers missed what its tool found.
Your playlists may soon get filtered.
Try the detector on your own music.
Is your library hiding fake tracks?

Try the detector on your own music.
Is your library hiding fake tracks?

instagram.caption:
Deezer scanned 100 million songs and found AI-generated tracks that had already been cleared by human moderators.

The tool didn't sample a few playlists—it swept every major streaming platform in one pass. Those tracks passed manual review, but Deezer's model spotted patterns our ears can't hear.

If you publish music, your tracks will soon need an authenticity check before reaching playlists.

Upload a track you suspect is synthetic to Deezer's public detector—what's the verdict in seconds 🧐
